summary: [APU Health Monitor] Create query for APU Health Monitor in Engine Folder

output_fields.name: psn
output_fields.description: Unique PSN associated with each APU Serial Number

output_fields.name: ref_date
output_fields.description: Reference Date (latest date only for each APU)

transformations: Join rotables table with rotables_trend table on psn field
Join result with aircraft table on ac_registr field to obtain aircraft type
For each APU (identified by psn), select only the row with the maximum ref_date (latest date only)
Retain event_perfno_i field as-is, allowing NULL values for APUs without associated workorders
acceptance_criteria: Query returns all APUs of type B737NG with their latest CT5ATP trend values
Each APU appears only once in results (latest ref_date per PSN)
All required fields are populated: partno, serialno, psn, ac_registr, ref_date, trend_type, event_perfno_i, value, ac_typ
Workorder field (event_perfno_i) can be NULL if no workorder is associated
VDS is created in Engine Folder
Query executes successfully against AMOS Acceptance database
Upon AMOS production go-live, data source can be switched to production without query logic changes
